seo.meta_title: Supabase Auth vs Kinde for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins?
seo.meta_description: Looking for Supabase Auth vs Kinde for your Next.js app? We compare pricing (Free – $75/mo vs Free – $300/mo), DX scores, setup time, and give you our honest pick.
seo.og_title: Supabase Auth vs Kinde — Which Next.js Auth Tool Wins?
seo.og_description: Supabase Auth scores 7/10 for Next.js DX. Kinde scores 7/10. See how they compare on pricing, features, and setup time.

hero.headline: Supabase Auth vs Kinde for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Supabase Auth and Kinde — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Both Supabase Auth and Kinde are solid choices for Next.js authentication, but they serve different needs. Supabase Auth edges ahead with a stronger Next.js integration score (7/10 vs 7/10). Where Kinde shines is Yes, up to 7,500 MAU. If you are optimizing for developer experience in the App Router, Supabase Auth is the safer bet.
verdict_text: For most Next.js developers building production apps, we recommend Supabase Auth. It offers better Next.js integration (7/10 vs 7/10), a easy learning curve, and a strong feature set that aligns well with App Router patterns. Kinde remains a solid choice — especially if yes, up to 7,500 mau is important to your stack.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

pros: Generous free tier with 7,500 MAU including most Pro features
Clean developer dashboard with visual workflow builder
Feature flags and remote configuration built into auth platform
Good Next.js SDK with documentation for App Router
cons: Next.js SDK is less mature than Clerk with fewer component abstractions
Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Advanced customization often requires self-hosting the hosted pages

faq.question: Should I choose Supabase Auth or Kinde for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Easy learning curve. Kinde scores 7/10 with a Easy learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Supabase Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier or Yes, up to 7,500 MAU is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Kinde more affordable?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. Kinde starts at Free – $300/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 7,500 MAU. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Supabase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Kinde easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Kinde has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Kinde is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Supabase Auth and Kinde together?
faq.answer: Using both Supabase Auth and Kinde in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Supabase Auth or Kinde?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.4★. Kinde has a Easy learning curve, 7/10, and 4.3★. On paper, Supabase Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
